instrument_sonnet: free-subagent sonnet-4.6 2AFC (the single attacker); A1 multiseed pool, delta=0.10
gate_rule: gpt-5.4 is already validated as a STRONG card-reader by the base 2nd-attacker run: its indiv positive control (per-person cards, UNCHANGED by the degeneracy fix, pooling-seed independent) is .711/.721/.655 (MAD/Enron/CV) >= sonnet and CI>0.5. That gate TRANSFERS to the fixed pooled cards here; so gpt-5.4 reading neutral/concat ~chance means the card is anonymous, not that the attacker is weak.
note: gpt-5.4 answers the SAME a1_ncc pairs sonnet did (byte-identical prompts). The win: gpt-5.4 reproduces A1 -> neutral CERTIFIED anon on all 3 (up95<0.60 for both poolers B & C); concat anon on MAD/Enron and (like sonnet) LEAKS on CV. That kills 'the pooled card is anonymous only because sonnet is a weak attacker'.
